pages.full_text:
We cooked at [?Churton] & had lunch next to the railway line in the carpark. We dropped of William & Joseph at Princes Risborough & spent a lovely sunny afternoon at Upton House. Amazing earth-moving in the garden. Oil magnate. Lovely coloured stone. Worth coming back! Home via Gaydon & M.40.

October 18th Friday
3.0. We left for south of Guilford, Rudgwick Riverside Farm ****  We have come down for Mylo Ruby's christening at Church of the Good Shepherd near Woking. We arrived at 8pm. On the way we spent hours in a traffic jam on the way to a Datchett petrol station.
     Rain in the night  October 19th.
     We set off at 10 south on the [~~Thames~~] Downs Link bike path, right next to the campsite. We went for [?a] mile to see the bridge [=] which is the Downs Link symbol.  The inspector said the incline was too much for the Train, so they built a metal bridge (1865) over
pages.notes: Top of page is continuation from previous spread. 'Churton' uncertain — could be a place name or personal name. 'Guilford' spelled as diary writes it (usually Guildford). 'Datchett' — diary spelling, normally Datchet (near Slough). The word struck through over 'Downs Link' appears to be 'Thames' with a strikethrough, corrected to 'Downs Link'. A small sketch of a bridge symbol (two lines with arch) appears inline in the text. Year not determinable from this page alone; volume covers 2003–2019, October entry.

pages.full_text:
the brick bridge. It existed for just nearly 100 years  Ahhh! Beeching!
It was a really nice ride. [?Used] at home in Surrey. At one point on the path we managed to collect nearly a bucket of sweet chestnuts.
     The ride was not easy. The path was muddy & slippery. But the autumn colours were beautiful & there were lovely views.
pages.notes: '[?Used]' — the word before 'at home in Surrey' is not entirely clear; could be 'Used' or 'Felt'. 'Beeching' refers to the Beeching cuts (1960s railway closures). Lower half of page is blank.
